description: Major trauma with hypotension after high-speed MVA in a 35-year-old male.

patient.name: James R., 35M
patient.chief_complaint: High-speed motor vehicle collision, polytrauma, ejected from vehicle. Open femur fracture, suspected pelvic fracture, decreased breath sounds on right with chest wall deformity. Active bleeding controlled with pressure.

rubric_notes: Major polytrauma with hemodynamic instability. ALL six vitals critical (SBP <80, HR >130, RR >30, SpO2 <90, temp <36, GCS ≤12). Suspected hemorrhagic shock + tension pneumothorax + open fracture. Per RUBRIC §1 Rule 1: severe shock with signs of hypoperfusion + major trauma with hypotension = ESI 1 ABSOLUTE. trauma_bay only. Massive transfusion protocol + trauma surgery consult immediately.
